name: Dr. Dre

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes strong evergreen streaming around the 2001-era catalog, producer and writer participation, and legacy label economics on the master side.

ownership.master_owner: Mix of label-controlled masters and artist-affiliated royalty participation
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing and writer participation remain a meaningful part of the economics
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumed in this estimate
ownership.notes: For producer-led catalogs, artist-side economics can remain strong because writing, production, and master participation all matter.

bio: Dr. Dre's catalog continues to earn through producer-led classics, enduring West Coast rap staples, and one of the strongest long-tail brands in hip-hop.
notable_for: Producer-driven rap classics, Dre-era sonic identity, and high-value catalog recognition across generations.
career_highlight: Still D.R.E. and The Next Episode remain anchor songs for 2000s rap playlists, nostalgia listening, and long-tail licensing value.
revenue_drivers: Producer-led catalog songs remain heavily replayed across rap and workout playlists.
Writing and production participation can materially improve the artist-side cut beyond performer royalties alone.
The catalog benefits from long-term cultural recognition and premium nostalgia demand.
